author: jih2nn
content:
Hey! Yes my Prysm implementation mostly focused on refactors that lifting off hard-coded slot time. It did't implement state transition and changes in the spec PR because I didn't want to bother state-related boilerplates and the PR was still half-baked. After discussing Preston, we found that we had a similar idea of incrementally merging those refactors into Prysm, although I'm not sure if it ever executed.

I'm also not sure if we're okay with merging that spec PR, especially when the plan is to make it adjustable first and then decide target reduced slot time. Carl would be the right person who could provide clearer vision than myself, but if I were to share my two cents, client teams could work on refactoring any design decisions based on the assumption that slot time is 12s and/or invariable in the meantime IMHO.

author: nflaig
content:
> client teams could work on refactoring any design decisions based on the assumption that slot time is 12s and/or invariable in the meantime IMHO
yeah, this was mostly what I had to fix in Lodestar
